result.lens: Random / extremal graph theory outside the lineage (random dense ring patches, freezing transitions of the 5-coloring measure, split-prime spindle groups)
result.first_principles:
A clamp is a totally frozen pair: a correlation that survives in EVERY proper 5-coloring. Statistical CSP theory knows exactly one generic source of totally frozen pairs: condensation of the solution measure as constraint density approaches the colorability threshold. L57 now has a structural reading: the lineage graphs are SAT-minimized, sitting at the BOTTOM of their chi=5 window (de Grey 1581: average degree 9.97), deep in the liquid phase where all forcing is adjacency-local. The missing chi-5 UDG must sit near the TOP of the window.

The only dial that raises UDG density is the rank of the unimodular group of the vertex ring (Erdos's unit-distance lower bound is exactly this multiplicativity). In Q(sqrt -3), Hilbert 90 gives an infinite-rank dial for free: u_p = pi_p/pi_p-bar over split primes p â‰¡ 1 mod 3 (u_7 = (1+4sqrt(-3))/7, rotation by arccos(1/7)). I built it and ran it. Result: density is NOT the Euclidean content. Saturated patches of Z[omega, 1/7] (n=1615, avg degree 14.2, triangle-rich) are 3-chromatic, and provably so at every density: unit-vector numerators have norm 49^k â‰¡ 1 mod 3, so reduction mod the ramified prime (1+omega) properly 3-colors the whole ring. Infinite incommensurable spindle rank, unbounded degree, chi=3 forever.

This unifies the detectors: Q^2's chi=2 (Woodall) is a 2-adic quotient coloring; my new ring is a 3-adic one; R^1 likewise. The abelian shadow of the problem is quotient colorings, and chi >= 5 territory is exactly the rings whose unit vectors kill EVERY finite quotient (the de Grey ring R3 does: omega_3 inverts 3). So the hunt must run inside quotient-dead rings at lineage-exceeding density: R3[1/7] = Z[omega, omega_3, 1/7], the lineage motor plus one foreign split-prime spindle.

Finally, the endgame needs repair: by L55's own monoid, a single realizable clamp cannot finish (J-I composes to J; chaining never contradicts). Cash-outs that do finish: forced-same at any distance D >= 1/2 (isoceles D,D,1 kill), or clamps at BOTH sqrt(3) and 2 (hexagon-plus-center, 7 points, 3 distances).

conjectures.name: Quotient-coloring completeness for finitely generated ring UDGs
conjectures.statement: For every finitely generated, conjugation-closed subring R of C whose unit-distance graph has finite chromatic number, chi(UDG(R)) = min over finite-index ideals I of R with U(R) âˆ© I = âˆ… of chi(Cayley(R/I, U(R) mod I)), where U(R) = {u in R : |u| = 1}. That is, the chromatic number of every algebraic ring's UDG is attained by an ideal-coset (periodic) coloring. Proven instances established this session: chi(Z[omega, 1/P]) = 3 for every set P of split primes p â‰¡ 1 mod 3, via reduction mod the ramified prime (1+omega), despite unbounded degree and infinite-rank incommensurable spindle group (script e7_splitprime_probe.py; SAT-verified on n=1615, avg degree 14.2). Woodall's chi(Q^2)=2 is the 2-adic instance.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Two concrete tests. (1) The conjecture demands an ideal-coset k-coloring of the full de Grey ring R3 = Z[omega, omega_3] with k = chi(R3) <= 7: enumerate ideals of norm <= 10^4, SAT-color the finite Cayley quotients; finding NO quotient <= 7-coloring strongly disconfirms. It also predicts no quotient 4-coloring exists (else contradict de Grey's chi >= 5); finding one would be an outright refutation of the conjecture or of arithmetic in the pipeline. (2) Any finitely generated ring patch whose SAT chromatic number strictly exceeds every quotient bound from ideals of bounded norm, with the gap persisting as the bound grows, is counterevidence.
conjectures.why_plausible: Every known exactly-determined algebraic case is a quotient coloring: Woodall's Q^2 (2-adic), Benda-Perles colorings of Z[i] distance graphs, the Eisenstein triangular lattice (mod (1+omega)), and now the split-prime localized rings at every density. No finitely generated ring is known whose UDG chi exceeds its best quotient bound. Compactness makes chi(R^2) the sup over f.g. rings, so completeness would mean the entire Euclidean content of Hadwiger-Nelson reduces to congruence obstructions on unit-norm elements: a local-global principle.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The interesting rings may be exactly where geometry beats all congruences: chi(R3) >= 5 already exceeds anything quotient colorings have delivered for that ring, and if chi(R3) = 5 or 6 with no coset witness at any ideal norm, completeness fails precisely at the frontier. Also the min over ideals may not be attained (descending chains), making the statement need a sup/limit repair.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Quotient upper bounds are classical (Woodall 1973; Benda-Perles; P.D. Johnson's colorings of abelian groups; scattered Polymath16 ring threads), but a COMPLETENESS principle (quotients always attain chi for f.g. rings) appears unstated anywhere. The flagship evidence object is also new: a UDG family with unbounded degree, triangles everywhere, infinitely many incommensurable spindle rotations, and chi pinned at 3. It is a detector-grade kill for every 'density + incommensurable rotations + odd cycles implies chi large' heuristic, a class of argument the literature has never explicitly closed.

conjectures.name: Freezing threshold in the quotient-dead rank-3 ensemble (clamp emergence)
conjectures.statement: Let R+ = Z[omega, omega_3, 1/7] (the de Grey ring extended by the foreign split-prime rotation u_7 = (1+4sqrt(-3))/7, which is NOT in the lineage's rotation monoid <zeta_6, omega_3>: its powers carry 7-denominators). Consider percolated random patches: G(q) = q-density random induced subgraph of (P510 âˆª tau + u_7Â·P510 âˆª saturation edges), swept over bindings tau and density q. Conditioned on chi(G)=5, there is a threshold d* in [12, 20] for average degree d such that for d > d*, G contains a non-adjacent forced-same or forced-different pair with probability bounded away from 0. The lineage's exhaustive freeness (L57) is the d <= 10 liquid phase of this same diagram, not a property of UDGs per se.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Among ~2000 percolation samples with n in [600, 3000] and average degree in [11, 20], conditioned chi=5 (guaranteed >= 5 on P510-containing samples), testing ~300 non-adjacent pairs per sample with the existing shotC/shotD double test: at least one non-free pair appears. Refuted at stated densities if all ~600k pair tests come back free, exactly extending L57's 2.29M-pair census into the dense out-of-lineage regime; that outcome would be major evidence for the omega <= 3 / chi = 5 world and should be logged as such.
conjectures.why_plausible: Random q-COL has a rigorous freezing phase (Molloy 2012; Zdeborova-Krzakala) starting near d â‰ˆ q ln q â‰ˆ 8-10 for q=5, and de Grey's graph sits at d = 9.97 yet liquid, consistent with geometric Kempe flexibility shifting the threshold up but not destroying it. The abstract clamp exists by a one-move construction (L51), so nothing combinatorial forbids the frozen phase. Dense 2D patches have automatically WIDE interfaces between distant regions, which is L55's only-live route (ii). The ensemble is quotient-dead (1/3 and 1/7 both inverted kill the mod-3 escape my probe found), removing the one mechanism proven this session to keep chi and forcing trivially low.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Geometric colorings may re-liquefy at ALL achievable UDG densities: the n^{4/3} unit-distance ceiling may cap d below the clustering-shifted freezing point, making 'no finite UDG is ever condensed' a theorem-shaped possibility. Bindings tau might generically fail to produce enough cross edges, so the realized d never reaches 12. Or freezing appears but only in samples whose chi has already jumped past 5 (empty conditioned window).
conjectures.novelty_claim: Importing the freezing/condensation phase diagram of random graph coloring into unit-distance ensembles, and predicting a clamped phase with a quantitative threshold, has to my knowledge never been stated (nearest art: de Grey's own informal probabilistic search heuristics, Polymath16 SAT-hardness anecdotes, and the spin-glass coloring literature, which never touches geometric graphs). The specific ensemble (lineage motor spindled by a split-prime rotation foreign to the lineage monoid) is also new; repo scripts e1f/e1g bind only within the rank-2 monoid.

conjectures.name: The pincer endgame: two tuned clamps finish, one clamp cannot
conjectures.statement: (a) Lemma-half, provable: a realizable forced-SAME pair at any distance D >= 1/2 implies chi(R^2) >= 6 (isoceles D,D,1: both endpoints of a unit edge equal the apex color). A single realizable forced-DIFFERENT clamp does NOT imply chi(R^2) >= 6 by contraction or chaining: contraction of distinctly-placed s,t is not a UDG operation, and by L55's monoid J-I relations compose only to J. But realizable clamps at pair-distances sqrt(3) AND 2 jointly give chi(R^2) >= 6: every 5-coloring would make 1, sqrt(3), 2 all bichromatic, yet the unit hexagon plus center is 7 points with pairwise distances in {1, sqrt(3), 2}. (b) Conjecture-half: Theorem R's center-distance D = |s* - t*| is a tunable flex parameter, and the first realizable clamp family admits realizations at both D = sqrt(3) and D = 2.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: (a) is brute-checkable now and a Lean target. (b) is falsified per-candidate: for any clamp candidate emerging from the freezing sweep, compute the flex of |s* - t*| over its realization space (numerics, plus exact Groebner on interface subsystems <= 14 vertices); if the flex interval of every found family excludes both sqrt(3) and 2, (b) fails and the program needs a third distance set (any finite 3-distance 6-point set {1, D1, D2} works as a substitute target, enumerable by exact algebra).
conjectures.why_plausible: The lemma-half is elementary and consistent with L56's splice lemma (forced-same is exactly clamp-strength). sqrt(3) and 2 are the two most orbit-rich distances of every hex-cored ring (rhombus diagonal and double edge), so candidate forced pairs at those distances are combinatorially favored in any hex-seeded ensemble; and Theorem R says D varies with the realization rather than being pinned by the abstract graph.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: If the program's intended cash-out was always 'realize the contracted H directly' (s* = t* case), the gap-flag is a misreading and only the repair's positive half matters. The flex of real clamps could be zero-dimensional (L53 caveat d: generically rigid H-w), pinning D at uncontrollable algebraic values. The hexagon lemma needs BOTH distances from clamps certified in the SAME 5-coloring universe, which is automatic, but two SEPARATE clamp graphs must both exist; doubling the existence burden.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The two-forbidden-distance literature (Exoo's eps-unit graphs, polychromatic number chapters in Soifer, Raiskii) studies chi with extra distances ASSUMED forbidden; using realizable clamps to FORCE the extra distances, with the hexagon-plus-center as the closer, and the explicit observation that one clamp under-delivers while two tuned clamps suffice, appears in neither the literature nor the repo (whose docs state 'contracting s=t then gives chi >= 6' without the realization caveat: this conjecture flags and repairs that step).

experiment.description: Foreign-spindle freezing sweep with forcing census (decisive for Conjecture 2, feeds 1 and 3). Stage 0 is already run and cached: e7_splitprime_probe.py established the split-prime ceiling (saturated Z[omega,1/7] patch n=1615, avg degree 14.2, chi=3 by SAT, matching the mod-(1+omega) theorem) and validated the Gaussian control (Z[i,1/5] patch bipartite). Main stage: (1) load P510 exact coordinates (e1i lineage scripts); (2) form W(tau) = P510 âˆª (u_7Â·P510 + tau) with u_7 = (1+4sqrt(-3))/7, sweeping tau over a small exact grid chosen to maximize cross unit-pairs (binding-search pattern of e1f/e1g, foreign rotation); coordinates live in the biquadratic field, edges verified by sympy-exact norm = 1; (3) percolate at q in {0.6,...,1.0}, ~50-200 samples; (4) SAT chi via cadical (chi >= 5 guaranteed by the P510 core; any 5-UNSAT sample is an instant headline and gets a second-solver reproduction); (5) for chi=5 samples, run the existing shotC (forced-same) + shotD (forced-different) double test on ~300 non-adjacent pairs each, prioritizing cross-copy pairs and pairs at exact distances sqrt(3) and 2 (the pincer distances); (6) side quest at idle cores: quotient sieve on R3, SAT-coloring Cayley(R3/I) for ideals of norm <= 10^3 (Conjecture 1's prediction that no 4-coloring and some <= 7-coloring exists). Decision tree: any non-free pair = first out-of-lineage forcing, escalate to pincer-distance tuning; all-free at d >= 12 = quantitative evidence for the chi=5 world, log as the dense-regime extension of L57.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Everything runs on the existing stack: python-sat/cadical (used in the probe today), sympy exact biquadratic arithmetic (lineage scripts already do this for P510), networkx, no C compiler needed. Reuses shotC/shotD pair-testing code and the e1f/e1g binding-search pattern. New code ~300 lines. Probe script and cache already at experiments/combinatorial/e7_splitprime_probe.py and _cache/e7_splitprime_probe.json.
experiment.est_cost: Stage 0: done (10 minutes). Binding construction and per-sample chi: seconds to minutes each. Forcing census dominates: ~300 pairs x 2 SAT solves x 1-5 s = ~30 min/sample; 50 samples in about a day of background CPU, 200 samples over a long weekend. Quotient sieve: a few hours. First decisive signal possible within hours since each sample is independently informative.

detector_self_check.Q2: Two independent layers. Mechanism: freezing of a q >= 3 coloring measure requires odd unit cycles, and every unit cycle in Q^2 is even (Woodall's bipartition), so the conditioned-on-chi=5 ensemble is EMPTY over Q^2; bipartite graphs provably have no forced non-adjacent pairs at q >= 3. Executed control: the Gaussian twin Z[i, 1/5] was actually run in the probe and verified bipartite (n=317, m=1304), so the pipeline cannot fabricate forcing from density. Strongest layer: the framework UNDER-proves where it should: on the quotient-rich ring Z[omega, 1/7] it returned the exact answer chi=3 (not a fake >= 5), and Conjecture 1 explicitly identifies Q^2's chi=2 as the 2-adic instance of the same quotient mechanism rather than an exception to be ignored.
detector_self_check.Linf: The density dial is the infinite-rank unimodular ROTATION group of the Euclidean circle (Hilbert 90: u = z/z-bar over split primes), which binds rigid congruent copies at incommensurable angles. The L^infty plane has no analog: its unit-ball symmetry group is the order-8 dihedral group, so u_7-style foreign spindling cannot be instantiated; moreover two L^infty unit circles meet in segments, so Theorem R's center-distance dial D = |s*-t*| (the pincer tuning parameter of Conjecture 3) is not even well-defined there. Finally all claims are conditioned on chi=5 subgraphs, and the L^infty plane has chi=4 exactly: the conditioning is empty, so nothing over-proves chi(L^infty) >= 5.
detector_self_check.R1: The unimodular group of R^1 is {Â±1}: rank zero. The ensemble degenerates to unions of arithmetic progressions, and after percolation to forests, whose coloring spaces never freeze for q >= 3 (trees are liquid at all densities). The freezing-threshold conjecture therefore predicts nothing on the line. The rotation group O(2) enters constitutively, not decoratively: the free rank driving density IS the arithmetic of the rotation group (z/z-bar parametrizes the circle), so the method is rotation-sensitive by construction.
